what_is_checked: Declared local test commands and latest recorded results.
why_it_matters: Shows whether the product was checked locally before the next workflow decision.
pass_meaning: Local test evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: At least one local test failed.
not_run_meaning: Local test evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Local test evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Local agent workflow create/status/index/report behavior and boundary checks.
why_it_matters: Shows whether dashboards and local automation can track advisory handoff state without provider execution.
pass_meaning: Agent workflow status evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Agent workflow status evidence failed or reported an unsafe boundary.
not_run_meaning: Agent workflow status evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Agent workflow status evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Product structure, required files, manifests, and product-local tools.
why_it_matters: Shows whether the repository can be maintained by the product workflow.
pass_meaning: Structure evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Structure check failed or required files are missing.
not_run_meaning: Structure evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Structure evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Canonical product documents and the workflow-state pair.
why_it_matters: Shows whether requirements, specification, implementation plan, task tracker, and handoff agree.
pass_meaning: Document sync evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Document sync evidence found a mismatch.
not_run_meaning: Document sync evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Document sync evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Product-local design-system source files.
why_it_matters: Shows whether future report UI design sources are valid and parseable.
pass_meaning: Design-system evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Design-system evidence found invalid or missing files.
not_run_meaning: Design-system evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Design-system evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Worktree, branch, upstream, ahead/behind, push, PR, merge, and local-remote synchronization.
why_it_matters: Shows where Git work should move next without guessing from a broad sync label.
pass_meaning: Git synchronization evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Git synchronization evidence reports a mismatch or blocker.
not_run_meaning: Git synchronization evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Git synchronization evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Current branch and uncommitted worktree changes.
why_it_matters: Shows whether local changes need commit, review, or cleanup before the next operation.
pass_meaning: Worktree evidence is current and clean.
fail_meaning: Worktree evidence found uncommitted or untracked changes.
not_run_meaning: Worktree evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Worktree evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Configured upstream branch for the current local branch.
why_it_matters: Shows whether local and remote comparison is possible without guessing.
pass_meaning: Upstream evidence is current and configured.
fail_meaning: Upstream evidence found a missing or invalid upstream.
not_run_meaning: Upstream evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Upstream evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Ahead and behind counts between the local branch and upstream.
why_it_matters: Shows whether push, pull/rebase, or no synchronization is the next Git concern.
pass_meaning: Local and remote synchronization evidence is current and matched.
fail_meaning: Local and remote synchronization evidence found ahead or behind commits.
not_run_meaning: Local and remote synchronization evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Local and remote synchronization evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Whether local commits have been pushed to a remote branch.
why_it_matters: Shows whether remote review or CI can observe the selected product revision.
pass_meaning: Push evidence is current and complete.
fail_meaning: Push evidence found unpushed commits or missing remote state.
not_run_meaning: Push evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Push evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Pull request existence and review target for the selected product revision.
why_it_matters: Shows whether review and PR CI can start from the current branch.
pass_meaning: PR evidence is current and ready.
fail_meaning: PR evidence found a missing, stale, or blocked pull request.
not_run_meaning: PR evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: PR evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Merge readiness and approval boundary for the selected product revision.
why_it_matters: Shows whether merge is allowed or still blocked by checks and approval.
pass_meaning: Merge evidence is current and ready.
fail_meaning: Merge evidence found a blocker or missing approval.
not_run_meaning: Merge evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Merge evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Pull request CI workflow, run status, conclusion, and head match.
why_it_matters: Shows whether PR checks belong to the selected product revision.
pass_meaning: PR CI evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: PR CI failed or does not match the selected product revision.
not_run_meaning: PR CI evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: PR CI evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Main branch CI workflow, run status, conclusion, and head match.
why_it_matters: Shows whether the observed CI result belongs to the selected product revision.
pass_meaning: Main CI evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Main CI failed or does not match the selected product revision.
not_run_meaning: Main CI evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Main CI evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: GitHub Actions provider visibility and latest relevant workflow run.
why_it_matters: Shows whether CI can be observed without treating missing provider data as success.
pass_meaning: GitHub Actions evidence is current and observable.
fail_meaning: GitHub Actions evidence is failed, unavailable, or mismatched.
not_run_meaning: GitHub Actions evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: GitHub Actions evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Verification policy, task ownership, execution profiles, resource boundaries, and CI graph consistency.
why_it_matters: Shows whether local and remote checks still cover every declared responsibility without unsafe duplication.
pass_meaning: Verification contracts are current and passed.
fail_meaning: The verification policy, ownership, or execution graph is invalid.
not_run_meaning: Verification contract evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Verification contract evidence no longer matches the current revision, inputs, or policy.

what_is_checked: Same-run package producer manifest and Node-version consumer results.
why_it_matters: Shows whether every package consumer tested the identical revision-bound tarball without repacking.
pass_meaning: The package artifact and every required consumer are current and passed.
fail_meaning: The package manifest, tarball digest, producer, consumer, or run identity did not match.
not_run_meaning: Package artifact evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Package artifact evidence belongs to another run, revision, input, policy, or toolchain.

what_is_checked: Proof-only terminal result for all required CI owners in one run and revision.
why_it_matters: Provides one unambiguous remote readiness result without rerunning provider suites.
pass_meaning: Every required CI owner passed and the final proof matches the current run and full HEAD.
fail_meaning: At least one required owner failed, was skipped, or produced mismatched proof.
not_run_meaning: Final CI proof has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Final CI proof belongs to another run, revision, graph, or policy.

what_is_checked: Atomic schema v2.2 receipts, complete clean exact-HEAD batches, bounded active admission and semantic retention, non-authoritative inactive history, integrity, policy-owned membership, parent-compatible projection, freshness, and manual-result authority.
why_it_matters: Prevents old, missing, mixed-batch, partial, cached, dirty, tampered, self-asserted, mismatched, concurrently lost, capacity-deadlocked, or improperly archived results from being shown as current PASS evidence.
pass_meaning: Evidence contracts are current and passed.
fail_meaning: Evidence authority, batch completeness, active retention, inactive archival, projection compatibility, integrity, freshness, redaction, migration, capacity, or concurrency checks failed.
not_run_meaning: Evidence contract checks have not been run yet.
stale_meaning: Evidence contract checks no longer match the current revision, inputs, policy, task graph, or release batch.

what_is_checked: Secret-bearing files, credentials, and unsafe disclosure risks.
why_it_matters: Shows whether work can proceed without exposing secrets or credentials.
pass_meaning: Security evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Security evidence found a blocker.
not_run_meaning: Security evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Security evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Local-first operation and artifact handling policy.
why_it_matters: Shows whether the product avoids external upload behavior by default.
pass_meaning: Local-first evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Local-first evidence found a blocker.
not_run_meaning: Local-first evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Local-first evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Browser profile isolation and profile reuse boundaries.
why_it_matters: Shows whether implementation avoids existing browser profile reuse by default.
pass_meaning: Browser profile evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Browser profile evidence found a blocker.
not_run_meaning: Browser profile evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Browser profile evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Local generated artifacts, ignored outputs, and non-source files.
why_it_matters: Shows whether local-only files are safely kept out of committed product source.
pass_meaning: Local artifact evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: Local artifact evidence found a blocker.
not_run_meaning: Local artifact evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Local artifact evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: External sending, OAuth, webhook, and integration data boundaries.
why_it_matters: Shows whether external communication is approved and safely scoped.
pass_meaning: External sending evidence is current and passed.
fail_meaning: External sending evidence found a blocker or missing approval.
not_run_meaning: External sending evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: External sending evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.

what_is_checked: Current security blockers that stop product operations.
why_it_matters: Shows what safety condition is actively stopping the workflow.
pass_meaning: Security blocker evidence is current and clear.
fail_meaning: Security blocker evidence found an active blocker.
not_run_meaning: Security blocker evidence has not been recorded yet.
stale_meaning: Security blocker evidence is older than the configured freshness window or product HEAD.
